title: ICE cotton closes higher as crude oil rises amid Middle East tensions
content:
Cotton rose as higher crude lifted polyester costs, enhancing cotton’s appeal as a substitute.
July settled at 79.45 cents.
Gains were capped by weak US export sales, lower China buying, profit booking and improved US crop conditions.
Geopolitical risks around the Strait of Hormuz supported sentiment, while Indian mills increased output on firm yarn demand.

title: China’s CNTAC keen on investing in Cambodia’s printing, dyeing sector
content:
The China National Textile and Apparel Council is keen on investing in Cambodia’s printing and dyeing industries, while committing to mitigating environmental pollution through advanced technology.  
The Cambodian Investment Board suggested an initiative to study zoning arrangements for such projects that would help ensure stronger environmental control and minimise potential pollution impacts.

title: India expected to breach FY27 budgeted fiscal deficit target: BMI
content:
India's fiscal deficit is likely to breach the FY27 budgeted target and hit 4.5 per cent of GDP as the government's policy response to the Middle East conflict could strain public finances, according to data analytics firm BMI. 
The FY27 budget had projected a 4.3-per cent fiscal deficit. 
BMI expects the government to introduce policies to restrain business costs and improve financial support for firms.

title: FY27 Bangladesh inflation to be above central bank target at 8.6%: BMI
content:
Inflation in Bangladesh is likely to reach 8.6 per cent in FY27 due to higher energy prices triggered by the Iran war, according to data analytics firm BMI. 
Inflation may remain above the Bangladesh Bank’s 6.5-per cent target set in its latest monetary policy, the Fitch Solutions company said. 
The central bank may keep the policy rate unchanged at 10 per cent in FY27 instead of cutting it, BMI said.

title: North India cotton yarn steady despite continued push by spinners
content:
North India cotton yarn prices remained steady as mills continued to push rates higher, supported by strong export orders and healthy margins.
Domestic demand stayed weak, with buyers limiting purchases to immediate needs.
Recycled yarn demand in Panipat remained subdued amid delayed export enquiries, while cotton prices eased in line with ICE cotton trends.

title: [Video] Inside a Seamless Luxury Stay at The Apurva Kempinski Bali With Samsung Hospitality TVs
content: Using Samsung Hospitality TVs and LYNK Cloud, The Apurva Kempinski Bali complements its five-star service with a seamless, personalized experience. Perched atop the cliffs of Nusa Dua, Bali, Indonesia, the luxury resort blends grand Balinese architecture, sweeping ocean views and a thoughtfully designed stay — where every detail, including in-room and on-property digital touchpoints, feels […]
